previous_statement: (Quantitative gap for finite-dimensional C*-algebras). For A = ⊕_{i=1}^N M_{d_i}(C), the face dimension spectrum is Spec(A) = {Σ_{i ∈ I} (r_i² - 1) + (|I| - 1) : I ⊆ {1,...,N}, 1 ≤ r_i ≤ d_i} which simplifies (using dim = Σ r_i² - 1 from the commutant) to Spec(A) = {s - 1 : s = Σ_{i ∈ I} r_i² for some I ⊆ {1,...,N}, 1 ≤ r_i ≤ d_i}. The smallest positive value in Spec(A) determines the gap. If all d_i ≥ 2, the smallest positive face dimension is min(d_1, ..., d_N)² - 1 ≥ 3 (achieved by a state supported on a single block with rank 2, giving face dimension 4 - 1 = 3). Wait: we must also consider states with |I| = 2 and all r_i = 1, giving Σ r_i² = 2, hence face dimension 1. Actually, this needs careful analysis: a state supported on two blocks with rank 1 in each has commutant ≅ C ⊕ C, dim_R(commutant^{sa}) = 2, face dimension = 1. So if N ≥ 2, face dimension 1 is achievable, and the gap shrinks. The gap {1, 2} only holds when the algebra is a single simple factor (N = 1) with dim ≥ 4 (i.e., d_1 ≥ 2).
new_statement: (Quantitative gap for finite-dimensional C*-algebras). [Depends on: node 1.9.1 (definition of Spec(A) and G(A) via face-commutant correspondence), node 1.1 (face-commutant correspondence).] For A = direct_sum_{i=1}^N M_{d_i}(C) with all d_i >= 2, the face dimension spectrum is Spec(A) = {(sum_{i in I} r_i^2) - 1 : empty set \!= I subseteq {1,...,N}, 1 <= r_i <= d_i for each i in I} union {0}. This follows from the face-commutant correspondence (node 1.1): a state phi supported on blocks I with rank r_i on the i-th block has commutant pi_phi(A)' isomorphic to direct_sum_{i in I} M_{r_i}(C), with dim_R(pi_phi(A)'^{sa}) = sum_{i in I} r_i^2, giving face dimension (sum r_i^2) - 1. The gap structure depends on N as follows. Case (a) N = 1 (simple algebra M_d(C), d >= 2): The smallest positive face dimension is d^2 - 1... wait, that is achieved by rank d; rank 1 gives dim 0 (pure), rank 2 gives dim 3. So the smallest positive face dimension is 3 (from r = 2), and G(A) contains {1, 2}. Case (b) N = 2 (two simple blocks, both d_i >= 2): Face dimension 1 is achievable via |I| = 2 with r_1 = r_2 = 1 (sum r_i^2 = 2, dim = 1). Face dimension 2 requires sum r_i^2 = 3, which needs either |I| = 1 with r^2 = 3 (impossible, r must be integer) or |I| = 2 with r_1^2 + r_2^2 = 3, only solvable by {r_1, r_2} = {1, sqrt(2)} (invalid). So 2 not in Spec(A), and G(A) contains {2} but not {1}. Case (c) N >= 3 (three or more simple blocks, all d_i >= 2): Face dimension 1 is achievable (|I| = 2, r_1 = r_2 = 1). Face dimension 2 is also achievable (|I| = 3, r_1 = r_2 = r_3 = 1, sum = 3, dim = 2). So G(A) contains neither 1 nor 2. Summary: (a) N = 1, d >= 2: G contains {1, 2}, automatic purity under m <= 2 constraints; (b) N = 2, all d_i >= 2: G contains {2} but not {1}, automatic purity under m <= 1 constraint only; (c) N >= 3, all d_i >= 2: G may not contain 1 or 2, no automatic purity guarantee from the gap alone. This is consistent with the corrected node 1.9.2(iv): Spec(M_2(C) + M_2(C)) = {0, 1, 3, 4, 7}, gap G = {2, 5, 6}.
